sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: B+, Moody's: B2, Fitch: B+

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Bangladesh's BNP won a landslide election on Feb 12, providing a new government mandate. A US-Bangladesh trade deal is in progress. GDP growth at 3.8% with 10% inflation. Oil price surge creates import cost pressure. Political stability improving under new government.
key_changes_this_week: BNP landslide election (Feb 12) provides new government mandate
US-Bangladesh trade deal negotiations in progress
Oil price surge adds inflation pressure
narrative.outlook: Bangladesh's new government has a mandate for economic reform and trade integration. Garment export sector remains the key driver. Oil import costs are a near-term headwind.
narrative.investor_implications: Bangladesh frontier market access limited. Garment sector benefits from China+1 sourcing. Taka under moderate pressure from oil costs. New government provides reform optionality.
narrative.data_quality_note: Bangladesh macro data is of medium quality. Election results confirmed.

active_alerts.title: Bangladesh BNP wins landslide in first election since 2024 uprising; policy shift expected
active_alerts.description: Bangladesh held its first post-Hasina election on Feb 12 with BNP winning a landslide (209 seats). Major democratic transition after 2024 uprising. Business leaders expressed optimism but sustained confidence depends on governance quality. Energy independence at 0.4 means oil surge exposure.
